summary: Added ArcBot, a desktop-only floating AI assistant backed by the local Codex CLI.
details: ArcBot checks local Codex install and sign-in status, offers an explicit first-time CLI install action, keeps the chat input editable during setup, supports moving/resizing the chat panel, adds a View menu option to show or hide the AI bot launcher icon, uses the ArcBot Mini PNG mark clipped by the circular launcher frame, lets the launcher icon be dragged and smoothly snapped to fully visible or halfway tucked positions at app edges, rotates the same image when tucked, keeps tucked edge/corner attachment and the icon-center offset from the nearest corner during window resize, hides the launcher while the chat panel is open, animates chat open/close transitions, shows compact labeled mode controls below a frameless auto-growing input, adds persisted local chat sessions with reopen/switch/new/history/archive/delete controls, shows a context panel with active-tab and estimated context usage details, presents progress as an in-chat work summary card that expands while running and collapses when complete, keeps raw Codex stdout/stderr in a copyable debug drawer, uses the configured Server Connection root path as the Codex project folder context, creates and uses %USERPROFILE%\Documents\ArcRho as the CLI working directory when the configured root is a network path, prefers npm/bundled Codex CLI shims over WindowsApps helper executables, avoids bundled .cmd path splitting by launching the Node entrypoint directly, can request context and the active in-memory method payload from the active DFM tab, lets Codex edit a local row-compact DFM method JSON temp copy, cleans accidental explanatory text around edited JSON before validation, and can apply host-validated JSON edits only inside the configured Server Connection root with a method-history backup, consistent row-compact JSON output, and latest-change revert.
